(born 17 July 1987) is an Australian former professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who played as a [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(rugby_league) "Fullback (rugby league)") for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL)[[3]](#cite_note-3) and has played for [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team") at international level and [State of Origin](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series") for [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team"). Boyd captained the Brisbane Broncos from 2017 to 2019.

**Darius Boyd** (born 17 July 1987) is an Australian former professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who played as a [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(rugby_league) "Fullback (rugby league)") for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL)[[3]](#cite_note-3) and has played for [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team") at international level and [State of Origin](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series") for [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team"). Boyd captained the Brisbane Broncos from 2017 to 2019.

Boyd previously played for the [St. George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St._George_Illawarra_Dragons "St. George Illawarra Dragons") in 2009–2011 and the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") in 2012–2014 all under coach [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)") before switching back to Broncos in 2015. Boyd has won the [2006 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2006_NRL_Grand_Final "2006 NRL Grand Final") with the Broncos and the [2010 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2010_NRL_Grand_Final "2010 NRL Grand Final") with the [Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons"). He is a Queensland State of Origin and [Prime Minister's XIII](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_XIII "Prime Minister's XIII") representative. Boyd also played as a [winger](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions") and [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions"), with the majority of his representative games played on the left wing. Boyd also played as a [five-eighth](/wiki/Five-eighth "Five-eighth") in 2019 for the Broncos.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Boyd was born at the [Gold Coast Hospital](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Hospital "Gold Coast Hospital"), in [Queensland, Australia](/wiki/Queensland,_Australia "Queensland, Australia") on 17 July 1987. He was raised by his single mother throughout most of his childhood.[[4]](#cite_note-origindebut-4)

He began playing junior football at a young age for the Parkwood Sharks, which led to playing at halftime during a [Gold Coast Seagulls](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Chargers "Gold Coast Chargers") match in 1993. He later switched clubs to the Mudgeeraba Redbacks.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Boyd attended Robina State High School during this time but switched schools to the famed rugby league nursery, [Palm Beach Currumbin High School](/wiki/Palm_Beach_Currumbin_High_School "Palm Beach Currumbin High School"), in Year 10. It was at this point Boyd's mother became ill and he started living with his grandmother, as well as spending periods of time living with football friends' families. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Following graduation in 2004, he was not recruited by any NRL teams and decided to repeat Year 12 at PBC in the hope of garnering attention from NRL teams. The decision turned out to be a fruitful one with the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") offering Boyd a scholarship, followed by a $20,000 contract for the [2006 NRL season](/wiki/2006_NRL_season "2006 NRL season"). [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Boyd was questioned by police in 2009 relating to an alleged sexual assault in a Brisbane nightclub.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Playing career")]

In 2005, while playing for Palm Beach Currumbin, Boyd was selected at fullback for the [Australian Schoolboys](/wiki/Australia_national_schoolboy_rugby_league_team "Australia national schoolboy rugby league team") team.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) He then debuted for the [Burleigh Bears](/wiki/Burleigh_Bears "Burleigh Bears") in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"). After playing for Burleigh, Boyd was signed by the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos"). He later said, "I was a bit scared of leaving home as an 18-year-old but Brisbane was always my favourite club since I was a little fella. I had to move into the Broncos' house and was a pretty shy, quiet kid so didn't want to leave the family and different things but after a week or two they made me feel welcome."[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### 2006

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2006")]

In round 1, Boyd made his [NRL](/wiki/NRL "NRL") debut for the Broncos, against the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys"),[[10]](#cite_note-10) scoring a try on debut in the Broncos 36–4 loss at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[11]](#cite_note-11) Playing on the wing for the majority of the season, Boyd got a chance at his preferred position of fullback for a number of games after the regular fullback, [Karmichael Hunt](/wiki/Karmichael_Hunt "Karmichael Hunt"), went down with injuries. At the end of the regular season, Boyd won the Broncos' Rookie of the Year award.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) Boyd played on the wing for the Broncos in their [2006 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2006_NRL_Grand_Final "2006 NRL Grand Final") 15–8 win over the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm").[[14]](#cite_note-14) Boyd played in 27 matches and scored 11 tries in an impressive debut year in the NRL.

### 2007

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2007")]

As 2006 NRL Premiers, the Brisbane Broncos travelled to England to face 2006 [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League") champions, [St Helens R.F.C.](/wiki/St_Helens_R.F.C. "St Helens R.F.C."), in the [2007 World Club Challenge](/wiki/2007_World_Club_Challenge "2007 World Club Challenge"). Boyd scored a try in the Broncos' 18–14 loss.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Boyd finished the [2007 NRL season](/wiki/2007_NRL_season "2007 NRL season") with him playing in 22 matches and scoring 7 tries.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Darius_Boyd_%28Brisbane_Broncos_vs._Canberra_Raiders_2008%29.jpg/250px-Darius_Boyd_%28Brisbane_Broncos_vs._Canberra_Raiders_2008%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Darius_Boyd_(Brisbane_Broncos_vs._Canberra_Raiders_2008).jpg)

Boyd while playing for the Broncos in 2008

### 2008

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2008")]

In Round 1 against the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers"), Boyd scored a hat-trick in the Broncos 48–12 win at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[16]](#cite_note-16) In May 2008, Boyd started to receive attention from other clubs as he was off-contract at the end of the year. There was speculation that he would move to the [Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs"), alongside fellow Broncos [Michael Ennis](/wiki/Michael_Ennis "Michael Ennis") and [Ben Hannant](/wiki/Ben_Hannant "Ben Hannant"),[[17]](#cite_note-17) or possibly the [St. George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St._George_Illawarra_Dragons "St. George Illawarra Dragons"), following his coach at Brisbane, [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)").[[18]](#cite_note-18) After leading the NRL try-scoring table for most of the first half of the season, Boyd was selected for the [Queensland team](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team") to play [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales_rugby_league_team "New South Wales rugby league team") in Game 2 of the [2008 State of Origin series](/wiki/2008_State_of_Origin_series "2008 State of Origin series") due to teammate [Justin Hodges](/wiki/Justin_Hodges "Justin Hodges") suspension.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Boyd scored 2 tries on debut, both from passes from centre [Greg Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis"), with Queensland winning 30–0 at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium")[[4]](#cite_note-origindebut-4) Boyd also played in Game 3 as the Maroons won 16–10 at [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/ANZ_Stadium "ANZ Stadium") and won the series 2–1.[[20]](#cite_note-20) On 20 August 2008, Boyd signed a 3-year contract with the Dragons starting in 2009,[[21]](#cite_note-21) following teammate [Nick Emmett](/wiki/Nick_Emmett "Nick Emmett") and coach Wayne Bennett. Boyd finished the [2008 NRL season](/wiki/2008_NRL_season "2008 NRL season") with him playing in 24 matches and scoring 13 tries. On 16 October 2008, Boyd was called up to the [Australian](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team") [2008 World Cup squad](/wiki/2008_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2008 Rugby League World Cup") due to teammate Justin Hodges being ruled out with a shoulder injury. On 9 November 2008, Boyd made his international debut for Australia against [Papua New Guinea](/wiki/Papua_New_Guinea_national_rugby_league_team "Papua New Guinea national rugby league team"), playing on the wing in the 46–6 win at [1300SMILES Stadium](/wiki/1300SMILES_Stadium "1300SMILES Stadium").[[22]](#cite_note-22) Boyd only played in 1 match in the World Cup tournament.

### 2009

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2009")]

In Round 1 of the [2009 NRL season](/wiki/2009_NRL_season "2009 NRL season"), Boyd made his club debut for the [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons") against the Melbourne Storm, playing at fullback in the 17–16 loss at [Olympic Park](/wiki/Olympic_Park "Olympic Park").[[23]](#cite_note-23)
In Round 4, he travelled to Brisbane for his and Wayne Bennett's first match against their old club, which the Dragons won 25–12, ending the Broncos unbeaten start to the season.[[24]](#cite_note-24) On 8 May, Boyd played for Australia in the [Anzac Test](/wiki/2009_Anzac_Test "2009 Anzac Test"), playing on the wing and scoring a try in the 38–10.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Boyd was selected in the Queensland squad for the [2009 State of Origin series](/wiki/2009_State_of_Origin_series "2009 State of Origin series"), playing in all 3 matches and scoring 1 try in the record-breaking 4th consecutive series win. In Round 20 against the [New Zealand Warriors](/wiki/New_Zealand_Warriors "New Zealand Warriors"), Boyd scored his first try as a Dragon in the 29–4 win at [Mt Smart Stadium](/wiki/Mt_Smart_Stadium "Mt Smart Stadium").[[26]](#cite_note-26) In August, Boyd's performance at a press conference, in which the 22-year-old fullback delivered one-sentence responses to eight questions posed to him at a training session in Wollongong, drew an apology to journalists from Dragons coach Wayne Bennett.[[27]](#cite_note-27) Boyd finished his first year with the Dragons with 2 tries in 22 matches.

### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2010")]

In Round 5 against his former team the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos"), Boyd played his 100th career match in the Dragons 34–16 win and was named Man of the Match at [WIN Stadium](/wiki/WIN_Stadium "WIN Stadium").[[28]](#cite_note-28) In Round 7, Boyd received the inaugural "Spirit of ANZAC Medal" as Man of the Match in the annual [ANZAC Day Cup](/wiki/ANZAC_Day_Cup "ANZAC Day Cup") in the Dragons 28–6 win against the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") at [Sydney Football Stadium](/wiki/Sydney_Football_Stadium_(1988) "Sydney Football Stadium (1988)").[[29]](#cite_note-29) Boyd played on the wing for Queensland in all three matches of the [2010 State of Origin series](/wiki/2010_State_of_Origin_series "2010 State of Origin series"), scoring a try in each match to become the series' top try-scorer. Towards the end of the season, Boyd was one of the favourites for the [Dally M Medal](/wiki/Dally_M_Medal "Dally M Medal"), having had a sizeable lead in points over the previous year's winner [Jarryd Hayne](/wiki/Jarryd_Hayne "Jarryd Hayne") and Sydney Roosters player [Todd Carney](/wiki/Todd_Carney "Todd Carney") when voting went behind closed doors after Round 16. He ended up finishing third, 2 points behind eventual winner Todd Carney and 1 point behind [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") hooker [Robbie Farah](/wiki/Robbie_Farah "Robbie Farah"). On 3 October 2010, Boyd played in the Dragons 2010 NRL Grand Final against the Sydney Roosters, playing at fullback in the Dragons 32–8 win and was awarded the Clive Churchill Medal as man of the match.[[30]](#cite_note-30) Boyd finished his successful [2010 NRL season](/wiki/2010_NRL_season "2010 NRL season") with him playing in 25 matches and scoring 2 tries. After the 2010 season, Boyd suggested that he may leave the Dragons if head coach Wayne Bennett did so.[[31]](#cite_note-31) On 4 October 2010, Boyd was selected in the Australia Kangaroos 24-man squad for the [2010 Four Nations](/wiki/2010_Four_Nations "2010 Four Nations").[[32]](#cite_note-32) Boyd only played in 1 match, playing against New Zealand, playing at fullback and scoring a try in the 34–20 win at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park").[[33]](#cite_note-33) Boyd won the 2010 [RLIF](/wiki/RLIF "RLIF") Fullback of the Year award in the off-season.

### 2011

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2011")]

Boyd played in the Dragons [Charity Shield](/wiki/Rugby_League_Charity_Shield_(Australia) "Rugby League Charity Shield (Australia)") 32–10 win over the [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs"), scoring a try while playing at five-eighth.[[34]](#cite_note-34) Boyd also played in the Dragons' [2011 World Club Challenge](/wiki/2011_World_Club_Challenge "2011 World Club Challenge") against the [Wigan Warriors](/wiki/Wigan_Warriors "Wigan Warriors"), playing at fullback in the 21–15 win at [DW Stadium](/wiki/DW_Stadium "DW Stadium").[[35]](#cite_note-35) In Round 1 against the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans"), Boyd equalled his 2009 and 2010 efforts with a double in the Dragons 25–16 win at [Robina Stadium](/wiki/Robina_Stadium "Robina Stadium").[[36]](#cite_note-36) On 30 March 2011, Wayne Bennett announced he would not coach the Dragons in 2012, hinting that Boyd may leave the club as well.[[37]](#cite_note-TVNZ_4094006-37)[[38]](#cite_note-38) After Bennett signed a 4-year contract with the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights"), Boyd was offered contracts from the Knights and the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans"). On 9 May 2011, the Titans officially withdrew their offer. On 9 June 2011, Boyd signed a 4-year contract with the Newcastle Knights starting in 2012.[[39]](#cite_note-39) Boyd played in all three matches of the [2011 State of Origin series](/wiki/2011_State_of_Origin_series "2011 State of Origin series") in the Maroons 6th consecutive series win. Boyd's final game for the Dragons was against his former club, the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium"). Boyd scored the try that forced golden point for the Dragons, after they were down 12–6, but the Dragons ultimately lost the match 13–12, after his test, state and former club captain, [Darren Lockyer](/wiki/Darren_Lockyer "Darren Lockyer") kicked the winning field goal.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Boyd finished the [2011 NRL season](/wiki/2011_NRL_season "2011 NRL season") with him playing in 22 matches and scoring 9 tries. On 16 October 2011, Boyd scored 2 tries for Australia in the 42-6 test match win against New Zealand at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[41]](#cite_note-41) Boyd was selected in the Australian squad for the [2011 Four Nations](/wiki/2011_Four_Nations "2011 Four Nations") tournament held in the United Kingdom. Boyd played all 4 matches and scoring 3 triesincluding playing at fullback in the Kangaroos 30-8 Four Nations Final win against [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_league_team "England national rugby league team") at [Elland Road](/wiki/Elland_Road "Elland Road").[[42]](#cite_note-42)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d9/Darius_Boyd.PNG/250px-Darius_Boyd.PNG)](/wiki/File:Darius_Boyd.PNG)

Boyd while playing for the Knights in 2012

### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2012")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Darius_Boyd_PM_XIII.jpg/250px-Darius_Boyd_PM_XIII.jpg)](/wiki/File:Darius_Boyd_PM_XIII.jpg)

Boyd playing for the Prime Minister's XIII in 2012

In Round 1 of the [2012 NRL season](/wiki/2012_NRL_season "2012 NRL season"), Boyd made his club debut for the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") against his former club the [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons"), playing at fullback in the Knights 15-14 golden point extra time loss at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[43]](#cite_note-43) On 20 April 2012, Boyd played for Australia in the [2012 Anzac Test](/wiki/2012_Anzac_Test "2012 Anzac Test"), playing on the wing in the Kangaroos 20–12 win [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park").[[44]](#cite_note-44) Boyd played on the wing for Queensland in all three matches of the [2012 State of Origin series](/wiki/2012_State_of_Origin_series "2012 State of Origin series"), scoring 3 tries in the Maroons 7th consecutive series win.

In Round 21 against the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders"), Boyd scored his first club try as a Knight in the 36–6 win at [Canberra Stadium](/wiki/Canberra_Stadium "Canberra Stadium").[[45]](#cite_note-45) Boyd finished his first year with the Knights with him playing in 22 matches and scoring 3 tries. On 23 September 2012, Boyd for [Prime Minister's XIII](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_XIII "Prime Minister's XIII") against [Papua New Guinea](/wiki/Papua_New_Guinea_national_rugby_league_team "Papua New Guinea national rugby league team"), playing at fullback in the 24–18 win at [Port Moresby](/wiki/Port_Moresby "Port Moresby").[[46]](#cite_note-46) On 13 October 2012, Boyd played on the wing for Australia in their 18–10 win over New Zealand.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2013")]

On 19 April 2013, Boyd played for Australia on the wing and scored a try in the [2013 Anzac Test](/wiki/2013_Anzac_Test "2013 Anzac Test") 32–12 victory.[[48]](#cite_note-48) He played all three games and was the top try scorer with 3 in the [2013 State of Origin series](/wiki/2013_State_of_Origin_series "2013 State of Origin series") in which Queensland extended their record for consecutive series victories to eight. Boyd played in 26 matches and scored 11 tries for the Knights. Selected in the 24-man Australian [2013 Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/2013_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2013 Rugby League World Cup") squad,[[49]](#cite_note-49) he played in 5 matches and scored 4 tries. Boyd played on the wing in the Kangaroos 34-2 World Cup Final win against the Kiwis at [Old Trafford](/wiki/Old_Trafford "Old Trafford").[[50]](#cite_note-50)

### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2014")]

On 2 May 2014, Boyd played on the wing for Australia in the [2014 Anzac Test](/wiki/2014_Anzac_Test "2014 Anzac Test"), in the Kangaroos 30–18 win at [Sydney Football Stadium](/wiki/Sydney_Football_Stadium_(1988) "Sydney Football Stadium (1988)").[[51]](#cite_note-51) In Round 13 against the [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium"), Boyd played his 200th career match in the Knights 23–20 loss at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[52]](#cite_note-52) Boyd played on the wing in all 3 matches of the [2014 State of Origin series](/wiki/2014_State_of_Origin_series "2014 State of Origin series"), scoring 3 tries in the Maroons first series defeat since 2005.[[53]](#cite_note-53)[[54]](#cite_note-54) On 23 July 2014, Boyd admitted himself to a [mental health](/wiki/Mental_health "Mental health") clinic to treat [depression](/wiki/Depression_(mood) "Depression (mood)").[[55]](#cite_note-55) On 14 August, Boyd completed his treatment for depression and came out about him admitting himself to rehab.[[56]](#cite_note-56) He didn't make a return to the Knights for the rest of the year as he continued to focus on his recovery. On 31 October 2014, Boyd was released from the final year of his Knights contract.[[57]](#cite_note-57) On 21 November 2014, Boyd signed a three-year contract with the Brisbane Broncos, returning to the club where he made his debut in 2006.[[58]](#cite_note-58) He finished the [2014 NRL season](/wiki/2014_NRL_season "2014 NRL season") with him playing in 14 matches and scoring 3 tries.

### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2015")]

On 5 December 2015, Boyd ruptured his [Achilles tendon](/wiki/Achilles_tendon "Achilles tendon") during pre-season training; this injury was expected to keep him out of action for at least six months, possibly seeing him sidelined for the entire 2015 season.[[59]](#cite_note-59) However, Boyd made a surprise return in Round 9 against the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers") at fullback in the Broncos 8–5 win at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[60]](#cite_note-60) Queensland coach, [Mal Meninga](/wiki/Mal_Meninga "Mal Meninga"), showing faith in Boyd to perform in Origin despite not playing the majority of the early [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season").[[61]](#cite_note-61) Boyd played in all 3 matches and scored a try for Queensland in the [2015 State of Origin series](/wiki/2015_State_of_Origin_series "2015 State of Origin series").[[62]](#cite_note-62) In Round 16 against his former club the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights"), Boyd scored his first try after returning to the Broncos in the 44–22 win at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[63]](#cite_note-63) In the Preliminary Finals match against the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters"), Boyd scored an intercept try in the first minute of the match after Roosters winger [Shaun Kenny-Dowall](/wiki/Shaun_Kenny-Dowall "Shaun Kenny-Dowall") attempted a 20-metre pass to fullback [Roger Tuivasa-Sheck](/wiki/Roger_Tuivasa-Sheck "Roger Tuivasa-Sheck"), the Broncos later won 31–12, sending them to the Grand Final.[[64]](#cite_note-64) On 4 October 2015, in the [2015 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2015_NRL_Grand_Final "2015 NRL Grand Final") against the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys"), Boyd played at fullback in the Broncos historic golden point 17–16 loss.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66) Boyd finished his return year with the Brisbane Broncos with him playing in 18 matches and scoring 3 tries in the [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season").

### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: 2016")]

On 1 February, Boyd was named in the Broncos' [2016 NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/2016_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2016 NRL Auckland Nines") squad.[[67]](#cite_note-67) Following a season-ending shoulder injury to his Queensland Origin team-mate [Billy Slater](/wiki/Billy_Slater "Billy Slater"), Boyd was selected to play Fullback for Australia against New Zealand in the [2016 Anzac Test](/wiki/2016_Anzac_Test "2016 Anzac Test"), scoring a try on the 16–0 win at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[68]](#cite_note-68)[[69]](#cite_note-69) With [Billy Slater](/wiki/Billy_Slater "Billy Slater") already out of action due to injury, Boyd filled the Fullback role for Queensland in the [2016 State of Origin series](/wiki/2016_State_of_Origin_series "2016 State of Origin series"), where he played in all 3 matches and scored 1 try in the Maroons series win.[[70]](#cite_note-70) In Round 14 against the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders"), Boyd scored a classy hat trick of tries in a space of 14 minutes in the Broncos 26–18 win at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium"). This was the first time Boyd scored a hat trick of tries since Round 1 of the 2008 season.[[71]](#cite_note-71)[[72]](#cite_note-72) Boyd finished the [2016 NRL season](/wiki/2016_NRL_season "2016 NRL season") with him playing in 25 matches and scoring 9 tries for the Broncos. On 20 September 2016, Boyd was added to the Kangaroos [2016 Four Nations](/wiki/2016_Rugby_League_Four_Nations "2016 Rugby League Four Nations") train-on squad.[[73]](#cite_note-73) On 4 October 2016, Boyd was selected in the [Australian Kangaroos](/wiki/Australian_Kangaroos "Australian Kangaroos") Final 24-man squad for the tournament.[[74]](#cite_note-74)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Darius_Boyd_Kangaroos.jpg/500px-Darius_Boyd_Kangaroos.jpg)](/wiki/File:Darius_Boyd_Kangaroos.jpg)

Boyd playing for the Kangaroos at [Anfield](/wiki/Anfield "Anfield") in 2016

Boyd played in 4 matches and scored 2 tries of tournament including starting at fullback and scoring a try in the Kangaroos 34-8 Four Nations Final win against New Zealand at [Anfield](/wiki/Anfield "Anfield").[[75]](#cite_note-75) On 7 October 2016, Boyd was awarded with the [Paul Morgan](/wiki/Paul_Morgan_(rugby_league,_died_2001) "Paul Morgan (rugby league, died 2001)") Medal as being the Broncos best-and-fairest player of the season.[[76]](#cite_note-76)

### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: 2017")]

Before the start of the 2017 season, Boyd joined the likes of [Darren Lockyer](/wiki/Darren_Lockyer "Darren Lockyer"), [Wally Lewis](/wiki/Wally_Lewis "Wally Lewis"), [Allan Langer](/wiki/Allan_Langer "Allan Langer") and [Gorden Tallis](/wiki/Gorden_Tallis "Gorden Tallis") when he was named the ninth Broncos captain in the club's 30-year history.[[77]](#cite_note-77) In February, he was selected in the Broncos [2017 NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2017 NRL Auckland Nines") squad.[[78]](#cite_note-78) His first game as captain of the Broncos ended with a 27–18 loss at [Halliwell Jones Stadium](/wiki/Halliwell_Jones_Stadium "Halliwell Jones Stadium") to the [Warrington Wolves](/wiki/Warrington_Wolves "Warrington Wolves") in the [2017 World Club Series](/wiki/2017_World_Club_Series "2017 World Club Series").[[79]](#cite_note-Warrington_27-18_Brisbane:_Wolves'_first-half_blitz_stuns_Broncos-79) In Round 3 against the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm"), Boyd played his 250th NRL career match in the Broncos 14–12 loss at [AAMI Park](/wiki/AAMI_Park "AAMI Park").[[80]](#cite_note-80)[[81]](#cite_note-81) On 5 May, Boyd played for Australia in the [2017 ANZAC Test](/wiki/2017_ANZAC_Test "2017 ANZAC Test") where he started at fullback in the 30–12 win.[[82]](#cite_note-82) Despite [Billy Slater](/wiki/Billy_Slater "Billy Slater") coming back from injury, Boyd retained his Maroons fullback role for Game 1 of the series.[[83]](#cite_note-83) However, in Game 2 Boyd was switched to centre to accommodate Slater's return to the fullback position.[[84]](#cite_note-84) In Round 26 against the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys"), Boyd injured his [hamstring](/wiki/Hamstring "Hamstring").[[85]](#cite_note-85) Scans revealed bad news for Brisbane with Boyd ruled out for the next 2 finals matches. In the preliminary final against the Melbourne Storm, Boyd made his return as the Broncos looked to make the grand final for the second time in three years.[[86]](#cite_note-86) Commentators believed Boyd wasn't fully recovered and replays saw him limping. In the second half, Boyd succumbed to his hamstring injury, with the Broncos eventually losing 30-0 and later missed selection for the Kangaroos [2017 Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/2017_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2017 Rugby League World Cup") squad.[[87]](#cite_note-87)[[88]](#cite_note-88) Boyd finished his [2017 NRL season](/wiki/2017_NRL_season "2017 NRL season") with 18 matches and 3 tries for the Broncos. On 26 October, Boyd extended his contract with the Broncos on a four-year deal, keeping him at Red Hill until at least the 2021 season.[[89]](#cite_note-89)

### 2018

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: 2018")]

On 11 June 2018, Boyd announced that he had retired from playing in representative matches after missing out on selection for Queensland. He commented, "My time's done in rep footy. I've had a great career, I have nothing to be disappointed about, I'm really happy with everything I've achieved in the rep arena. I don't think I have anything more to prove, it's time for the younger fellas coming in, they have great players, really quality guys coming through."[[90]](#cite_note-90) Between Rounds 19–21, Boyd played at centre as coach Wayne Bennett experimented playing youngster [Jamayne Isaako](/wiki/Jamayne_Isaako "Jamayne Isaako") at fullback.[[91]](#cite_note-91)[[92]](#cite_note-92) Boyd finished the [2018 NRL season](/wiki/2018_NRL_season "2018 NRL season") with him playing of the 25 matches and scoring 5 tries for the Broncos.

### 2019

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: 2019")]

In Round 8 against the [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs"), Boyd played his 300th NRL career match, playing his milestone game against his old mentor, Rabbitohs coach [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)") who Boyd spent his whole career being coached by him until the 2019 season when Bennett was given the sack by the Broncos and swapped clubs with [Anthony Seibold](/wiki/Anthony_Seibold "Anthony Seibold").[[93]](#cite_note-93) The Broncos lost the match 38–6 at [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/ANZ_Stadium "ANZ Stadium") as the Rabbitohs dedicated the win to the recently retired Rabbitohs star [Greg Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis").[[94]](#cite_note-94) Boyd has copped plenty of criticism for his form in the early rounds of the 2019 season, practically his defence and leadership. Boyd commented, "I don't know what the outside noise is saying, but my body is really good and my form, I'm pretty happy with it. "I spoke to 'Seibs' (Broncos coach Anthony Seibold) and he's happy with how I'm performing, "I know if we win some games the critics will go away. "I can be better and I'm not saying I don't have to put my hand up and be better, because I know I do, especially as a captain, but I'm not losing sleep over my performances".[[95]](#cite_note-95)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bb/Darius_Boyd_Brisbane_Broncos.jpg/500px-Darius_Boyd_Brisbane_Broncos.jpg)](/wiki/File:Darius_Boyd_Brisbane_Broncos.jpg)

Boyd playing for the Broncos in 2019

On 30 August 2019, Boyd was selected at Wing for the Queensland Maroons Team of the Decade.

At the end of the 2019 regular season, Brisbane finished in 8th place on the table and qualified for the finals. Boyd captained the club in their elimination final against Parramatta which Brisbane lost 58–0 at [Western Sydney Stadium](/wiki/Western_Sydney_Stadium "Western Sydney Stadium"). The defeat was, at the time, Brisbane's biggest ever loss and the biggest loss in finals history which eclipsed Newtown's record when they defeated St George 55–7 in 1944.[[96]](#cite_note-96)[[97]](#cite_note-97)[[98]](#cite_note-98)

### 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: 2020")]

On 6 March 2020, Boyd announced that he would retire at the end of the [2020 NRL season](/wiki/2020_NRL_season "2020 NRL season") despite having one year left to run on his contract.[[99]](#cite_note-99) He was the final member from the club's [2006 premiership team](/wiki/2006_NRL_season "2006 NRL season") to retire.[[100]](#cite_note-100)

In round 20 of the [2020 NRL season](/wiki/2020_NRL_season "2020 NRL season"), Boyd sustained a career-ending injury against rivals North Queensland at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium"). Boyd scored a first half try as the Broncos lost the match 32–16.[[101]](#cite_note-101)

## Post playing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Post playing")]

On 4 October 2024, Boyd revealed that he had departed the Broncos after the exit of former coach Kevin Walters. Boyd had been working with the teams outside backs as a mentor.[[102]](#cite_note-102)

On 5 February 2025, the Queensland Maroons announced that Boyd had been named the coach of the Queensland Under 19 men's team.[[103]](#cite_note-103)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darius_Boyd&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Boyd married Kayla Boyd in 2012.

In 2014, he spent three weeks in a private psychiatric hospital, located in Western Sydney, being treated for depression, after his wife Kayla Boyd temporarily left him. He quit the Knights mid-season. Boyd left after spending 3 weeks there.[[104]](#cite_note-104)

In 2017, Kayla Boyd had a miscarriage before falling pregnant four months later and [terminating that pregnancy](/wiki/Abortion "Abortion") because she "wasn't ready".[[105]](#cite_note-105)

The Boyd’s first daughter was born on September 26, 2015.[[106]](#cite_note-106) Second daughter was born on September 28, 2019.[[107]](#cite_note-107)[[108]](#cite_note-108) Their third daughter was born March 15, 2021.[[109]](#cite_note-109)

Boyd featured on the third season of the [Seven Network](/wiki/Seven_Network "Seven Network")'s reality quasi-military training television programme *[SAS Australia: Who Dares Wins](/wiki/SAS_Australia:_Who_Dares_Wins "SAS Australia: Who Dares Wins")*, which premiered on 21 February 2022.[[110]](#cite_note-110)
